changes.before: palaces kept their dignity in the front rooms and their clutter where the work got done
changes.after: front rooms stayed polished while clutter gathered where the work got done

changes.before: His eyes found Jaina, the corridor behind her, then the cold shivering through the stones.
changes.after: He took in Jaina, the corridor behind her, then the cold shivering through the stones.

changes.before: His eyes found Jaina, the corridor behind her, then the cold shivering through the stones.
changes.after: He took in Jaina, the corridor behind her, then the cold shivering through the stones.

changes.before: For one thin second the corridor belonged to people who understood it.
changes.after: For one thin second, no one in the corridor needed anything explained.

changes.before: Not saved. Not fixed. Done.
changes.after: Done.

changes.before: The misery of him was that he never needed truth softened.
He took the blow because she was right.
changes.after: He said nothing.

changes.before: Sylvanas took that in.
changes.after: Sylvanas saw the blood on Jaina's hand.

changes.before: Kings liked a white that let distance do half the ruling for them.
changes.after: White marble stretched between the service door and the throne.

changes.before: the body remembering its shape
changes.after: the flesh pulled itself closed, slow and obscene, reshaping around the wound

changes.before: a sword that believed for half a second it had only one enemy
changes.after: a sword fixed on one enemy for half a second

changes.before: His eyes found her completely then
changes.after: Then he turned on her fully, and the violence in his face told her the theory had been right.

changes.before: Not loud. Not human. The sound came from farther back than his throat.
changes.after: The sound came from farther back than his throat, low and inhuman.

changes.before: Not invulnerable. Just held together by appetite with no mercy in it.
changes.after: Held together by appetite with no mercy in it.

changes.before: Not broken. Not yet. But the rhythm tore open.
changes.after: The blade stayed whole, but the rhythm tore open.

changes.before: a place a storm had chosen to keep
changes.after: a place gone storm-struck

changes.before: Every spell chosen for drag, not glory.
changes.after: Every spell chosen to slow him.

changes.before: Winter held the room in both hands.
changes.after: The room sat deep in winter cold.

changes.before: No miracle marked it. No banner of light. No choir for murdered kings and ruined sons.
changes.after: The kill came without ceremony.

changes.before: For one impossible instant she had him.
changes.after: She had him.

changes.before: Not restored. Not forgiven. Only emptied enough by pain and shock that the old lines showed through.
changes.after: Only emptied enough by pain and shock that the old lines showed through.

changes.before: Boyhood at the eyes.
changes.after: Boyhood in his face.

changes.before: The chamber gave them a worse shape of it.
changes.after: What they saw was worse.

changes.before: The room gave them a worse shape of it.
changes.after: What they saw was worse.

changes.before: Their eyes took it one piece at a time.
changes.after: They took it in one piece at a time.

changes.before: Their fear had already chosen its story.
changes.after: They had already chosen their story.

changes.before: Then his gaze found Arthas.
changes.after: Then he turned to Arthas.

changes.before: His eyes lifted to Jaina. Then to Sylvanas. Then to Frostmourne.
changes.after: He checked Jaina, then Sylvanas, then Frostmourne.

changes.before: His eyes lifted to Jaina. Then to Sylvanas. Then to Frostmourne.
changes.after: He checked Jaina, then Sylvanas, then Frostmourne.

changes.before: Not much. Enough.
changes.after: Enough.

changes.before: the truth stood between them in full armor
changes.after: the facts were plain between them

changes.before: Not witnesses. Not with Terenas dead, Arthas dead by an arrow, and Sylvanas Windrunner standing in the throne room of Lordaeron.
changes.after: Witnesses were impossible with Terenas dead, Arthas dead by an arrow, and Sylvanas Windrunner standing in the throne room of Lordaeron.

changes.before: Not questioning. Measuring.
changes.after: Measuring.

changes.before: Not because she trusted the room. Not because surrender tasted noble. Because neither of them had enough left to cut through thirty men and a ring of paladins while a damaged soul-drinking sword twitched at their feet.
changes.after: Because neither of them had enough left to cut through thirty men and a ring of paladins while a damaged soul-drinking sword twitched at their feet. Because living long enough to speak later, as Uther had told her in the corridor, was the only work left that might still matter.

changes.before: The chamber had become a ruined altar to several incompatible truths.
The chamber had become a ruined altar to several incompatible truths.
The look she gave him promised excellent reasons for regret if either of them ever walked free again.
changes.after: She turned her head enough to let him know she would remember him if either of them ever walked free again.

changes.before: Not close enough to be comfort. Close enough that no frightened man would decide a bound prisoner needed the back of a spear while moving.
changes.after: Close enough that no frightened man would decide a bound prisoner needed the back of a spear while moving.

changes.before: Palaces hid disaster the moment the doors shut.
Palaces hid disaster the moment the doors shut.

Not from the sword this time. From the actual kitchens somewhere below, where dough would already be rising for the evening meal because kingdoms, even broken ones, kept feeding themselves while they decided whom to blame.
changes.after: From the actual kitchens somewhere below, where dough would already be rising for the evening meal because kitchen fires stayed lit while rulers decided whom to blame.

changes.before: kingdoms, even broken ones, kept feeding themselves while they decided whom to blame
changes.after: kitchen fires stayed lit while rulers decided whom to blame
